idea.hook: Which planet in our solar system has the craziest, fastest weather?
facts: Neptune, the outermost gas giant, holds the record for the fastest known winds in our Solar System.
These incredibly powerful atmospheric currents can reach speeds exceeding 2,100 kilometers per hour (1,300 mph).
For comparison, that's nearly twice the speed of sound on Earth (approx. 1,235 km/h or 767 mph)!
idea.payoff: Imagine a world where storms are literally supersonic, faster than a jet plane!
idea.title: Neptune: Supersonic Storms! 💨🤯

scenes.visual: A vibrant, swirling galaxy background. A large question mark subtly pulses in the center.
A majestic, deep blue Neptune rotating slowly against a starfield. Subtle atmospheric currents are visible.
Close-up of Neptune's stormy, banded atmosphere. Streaks indicate rapid wind movement.

scenes.text: Nearly TWICE the speed of sound on Earth!
scenes.visual: Split screen: left shows a stylized Earth with a sonic boom effect; right shows a turbulent section of Neptune's atmosphere.

scenes.text: Supersonic storms: FASTER than a jet plane!
scenes.visual: An impactful animation showing powerful, swirling winds on Neptune, perhaps with visual 'speed lines' or a subtle shockwave effect highlighting the extreme velocity.
